name: Whole Grain Blueberry Lemon Muffins
directions: Preheat oven to 375 degrees.
Lightly spray muffin tin with non-stick spray.
Mix flour, sugar and baking powder in large mixing bowl.
In a separate bowl, combine eggs, yogurt, and applesauce.
Stir into dry ingredients and mix lightly.
Fold in blueberries.
Bake 18-20 minutes or until muffin tops are browned.
Loosen muffins.
Serve warm and enjoy!

name: Delicious Blueberry Muffins
directions: Sift first 4 ingredients twice.
Add mil & beaten egg to melted butter.
Combine with dry ingredients, mixing just enough to moisten.
Lightly fold in blueberries & fill well greased muffin tins no more than 2/3 full.
Bake at 400F about 20 minutes or until lightly brown.

name: Blueberry Buckle
directions: In bowl, cream together granulated sugar with butter; beat in egg and vanilla. Stir together flour, baking powder and salt. Stir half of the flour mixture into butter mixture; blend in milk and remaining flour mixture to make stiff batter.
Spread batter in greased 8-inch (2 L) square cake pan. Cover evenly with blueberries.

Topping: In small bowl, stir together brown sugar, flour and cinnamon. Cut in butter until mixture is crumbly. Sprinkle evenly over blueberries. Bake in 350°F (180°C) oven until crisp and browned on top and knife inserted in center comes out clean, about 40 minutes. Serves 6-8.
Canadian Living.

name: Blueberry Bran Muffins
directions: Preheat oven 400 F.
Stir flours, baking powder, soda, and salt together, set aside.
In a large bowl, stir together cereal, milk and juice.  Let stand 3 minute or until cereal softens.  Add egg, oil and sugar. Beat well.
Add flour mixture, stirring just until combined. Fold in berries. Spoon batter evenly into lightly greased or paper-lined muffin tin.
Bake at 400 F 12-14 min or until golden brown and tops of muffins spring back when lightly touched.

name: Chocolate Blueberry Cake
directions: Preheat oven to 350°F
In a medium bowl, mix flour, cocoa powder, baking powder, baking soda, ground flax, and salt.
In blender, combine water, 1/2 cup blueberries, and balsamic vinegar and blend until smooth.
Make a well in the dry ingredients.  Add the syrup and the blueberry mixture. Stir until completely mixed.
Pour into an oiled 9-inch round cake pan. Bake 30 minutes or until a toothpick inserted in center comes out clean. Cool completely before inverting onto serving platter.
Serve with blueberries on top, drizzled with additional syrup or agave.

name: Very Berry Muffins
directions: preheat oven to gas mark 4 180 C /355°F.
Mix together flour, baking soda, baking powder and salt.
Mash, chop the berries coarsely and add to the dry ingredients.
Whisk the egg, stir in the sugar, oil and yoghurt. Fold in the dry ingredients and stir briefly.
Spoon mixture into the muffin tins, bake in centre of the oven for 20-25 minutes.
leave the muffins for 5-10 mins in the tin, take out of the tin and leave until cooled serve with custard.

name: Yogurt and Berry Muffins
directions: Preheat your oven to 200oC and line 12 muffin tins with muffin cases.
In a large bowl, eat the eggs until frothy.
Gradually add the sugar while beating and slowly pour in the oil while still beating.  Add the extract and beat to incorporate.
Add in the yogurt and beat until smooth.  (If your yogurt is overly sweet, you might like to reduce the sugar amount).
Add in the flour and berries, carefully fold inches  Don't over mix as the muffins won't rise very high and will be more stodgy than fluffy.
Scoop  batter into prepared muffin cups with a large tablespoon.
Bake in your preheated oven for 18-22 minutes.
Don't eat until slightly cooled - the berries are hot!

name: Lemon Raspberry Muffins
directions: Preheat oven to 400°F Coat 12 large (1/2-cup) muffin cups with cooking spray or line with paper liners.
Use a vegetable peeler to remove the zest from the lemon in long strips. Combine the zest and sugar in a food processor; pulse until the zest is very finely chopped into the sugar.
Add yogurt, oil, egg, and vanilla. Pulse until blended.

Combine whole-wheat flour, baking powder, baking soda, and salt in a large bowl. Add the yogurt mixture and fold until almost blended. Gently fold in raspberries. Divide the batter among the muffin cups.
Bake the muffins until the edges and tops are golden, 18 to 25 minutes. Let cool in the pan for five minutes before turning out onto a wire rack. Serve warm.
